description: GST composition scheme rates under Section 10 of the CGST Act, for small taxpayers below turnover thresholds
source: Section 10 of CGST Act 2017; CBIC Notification No. 8/2017-Central Tax (and subsequent amendments); Section 10(2A) for composition scheme for services introduced by Finance Act 2019
notes: Composition dealers cannot collect tax from customers and cannot claim input tax credit. They must display 'Composition Taxable Person — not eligible to collect tax' on every invoice. They file CMP-08 quarterly and GSTR-4 annually. Thresholds: ₹1.5 crore aggregate turnover for goods (₹75 lakh for special category states), ₹50 lakh for services under 10(2A). Rates are split equally between CGST and SGST.

rates.description: Composition rate for traders (including manufacturers from 1 April 2018 after rate unification)
rates.applicability: Traders and manufacturers under Section 10(1). Rate applies to aggregate turnover of taxable supplies of goods.

rates.description: Composition rate for restaurants and catering
rates.applicability: Restaurants not serving alcoholic liquor, eligible under Section 10(1)

rates.description: Composition rate for services and mixed suppliers under Section 10(2A)
rates.applicability: Service providers and mixed suppliers (goods + services) with aggregate turnover up to ₹50 lakh, eligible under Section 10(2A) introduced by Finance Act 2019
